description: Inside Central Perk, Phoebe sits on the orange couch reading a purple book while Rachel sits beside her. Instrumental music plays. Ross enters from the background. "Hey," Ross says as he approaches.
Ross stops in front of the couch. Phoebe and Rachel look up at him.

Rachel stands and puts her hands on her waist. "So did everything go okay with the annulment?" she asks. Ross gestures with his hands, "Oh, yeah. No problems. It's all taken care of." Rachel clasps her hands together. "Ross, thank you," she says, then embraces him.
Ross and Rachel release their hug. Phoebe continues to watch them from the couch, her book still resting in her lap.
Rachel looks between Phoebe and Ross. "Hey, you guys want to go see a movie?" she asks. Ross nods and says, "Oh, yeah."
Ross adds, "Why not?" Rachel turns and begins walking toward the back of the shop. "I'm going to get my sweater," she says.
Ross watches her walk away. "Okay," he says.
Rachel exits the scene. Ross sits on the orange couch next to Phoebe. He leans in close to her while she turns her head to look at him.
Ross maintains eye contact with Phoebe. "You want to hear something weird?" he asks, his volume lower than his previous line.
Phoebe turns her body fully toward him. "Always," she replies.
Ross looks over his shoulder toward the back of the shop, then turns back to Phoebe.
Ross leans in closer to Phoebe. "I didn't get the annulment," he says in a soft voice.
Phoebe remains still, her eyes wide and her eyebrows raised. Audience laughter is audible.
Phoebe asks, "What?" her eyebrows drawing together.
Ross nods his head slightly. "We're still married," he says.
Phoebe stares at him with her mouth slightly parted. Ross maintains a steady gaze on her.
Ross stands up and begins to walk away from the couch. "Don't tell Rachel. See you later," he says.
Phoebe remains on the couch, looking in the direction Ross went. Outside the window, Rachel is visible walking past on the sidewalk, now wearing a black sweater over her blue top.
Now outside the storefront at night, Ross and Rachel walk down the sidewalk together. "Hey, do you have any gum?" Ross asks.
Rachel reaches into her purse. "Oh," she says, stopping her movement.
Phoebe runs out of the coffee shop door and toward them on the sidewalk.
Phoebe holds up one hand. "Wait," she says.
Phoebe reaches out and touches Ross's arm. "Hi. Listen, Ross, can I just talk to you for just a second?" she asks.
Rachel looks down at her purse. "Oh, but Phoebe, we're gonna be late for the movie," she says. Phoebe looks down the street and raises her arm. "Oh, there's a cab. Taxi!" she shouts.
A yellow taxi pulls up to the curb and stops. Ross and Phoebe watch the car.
Phoebe ushers Rachel toward the taxi. "Timing. My God. There you go," she says. Ross reaches out to help open the door.
Rachel gets into the back seat of the car. Phoebe says, "There you go." Rachel leans out the window and says, "We're actually just going to walk because it's right up there at the Angelica."
Phoebe slaps the roof of the taxi with her hand. "Oh, the Angelica. Go, go!" she shouts.
The taxi drives away. Rachel is visible through the rear window. A sharp whistle sound is heard.
Phoebe turns to face Ross. "You didn't get the annulment?" she asks, her volume louder than before. Ross looks at her. "I know," he says.
Phoebe continues to look at him, waiting for a further response.
Ross gestures with both hands as he speaks. "Well, I tried, but when I got to my lawyer's office, all I could hear was 'Three divorces. Three divorces!'" he says.
Ross mimics a high-pitched singing voice for the repeated phrase. Phoebe stands still with her hands on her hips. "Yes, in that high voice," Ross adds.
Ross continues speaking, his hands moving in front of him. "Look, I just don't want my tombstone to read 'Ross Geller, three divorces,'" he says.
Phoebe keeps her hands on her waist, looking directly at Ross.
Phoebe says, "Don't be worried about that. Your tombstone could say whatever you want it to say. It can say, 'Ross Geller, good at...'"
Phoebe pauses her speech and looks upward briefly.
"...marriage.' You know?" Phoebe adds, looking back at Ross.
Phoebe shifts her gaze away from him. "Mine's going to say 'Phoebe Buffay, buried alive,'" she says.
Ross looks at Phoebe without speaking.

Ross raises both hands and gestures. "All I know is I can't have another failed marriage," he says. Phoebe steps closer to him. "So, okay. What? You're going to be married to a girl who doesn't even know about it?" she asks.
Phoebe quickly adds, "Woman. Sorry."
Ross looks down at the ground and then back up at Phoebe.
Ross gestures toward his chest. "Okay, so I don't have it all worked out quite yet. Just don't say anything to Rachel, please," he says.
Phoebe maintains her posture with her hands on her hips, looking at him.
Phoebe points to herself. "Rachel is one of my closest friends," she says.
Phoebe continues, "Although being the only one that knows about this does make me feel..." She pauses and a small smile appears on her face.
The yellow taxi returns and stops at the curb. Phoebe and Ross turn their heads to look at it.
"...special," Phoebe finishes. Rachel leans her head out of the taxi's passenger window.
Phoebe leans toward the taxi. "Hey, Rach!" she says. Rachel looks at them and asks, "What was that?" Phoebe replies, "Sorry, mix up."
Rachel looks between Phoebe and Ross with her head tilted to one side.
Phoebe leans her arms over the taxi door. "Hey, how's the movie?" she asks.
Rachel looks forward through the front windshield of the taxi.
Rachel turns her head back to Phoebe. "I haven't seen it yet," she says. Phoebe gestures down the street. "Well, then you better hurry. The Angelica. Go, go!" she says.
Phoebe slaps the roof of the taxi and it drives away. Rachel shouts, "No!" as the car moves. Ross puts a piece of gum into his mouth.
The scene changes to a city skyline at night. A graphic of a fountain and an orange couch appears with the word "FRIENDS." Upbeat music with vocals begins.
Six people dance in and around a large fountain at night. The name "JENNIFER ANISTON" appears over clips of Rachel laughing and dancing.
The name "COURTENEY COX ARQUETTE" appears over clips of Monica reading a book on a couch and dancing in the fountain.
The name "LISA KUDROW" appears over clips of Phoebe waving and dancing. A group shot shows the cast members splashing in the fountain.
The name "MATT LE BLANC" appears over clips of Joey smiling and standing in the water. The cast continues to dance together.
The name "MATTHEW PERRY" appears over clips of Chandler talking and dancing. The name "DAVID SCHWIMMER" appears over Ross smiling and falling into the fountain water.
Joey and Chandler embrace while sitting on a chair. Monica and Chandler look at each other while kneeling. The cast sits together on the orange couch.
The cast members sit on the couch in front of the fountain. Text reads "CREATED BY DAVID CRANE & MARTA KAUFFMAN." The music concludes.
An exterior view of an apartment building with fire escapes under a blue sky. Upbeat instrumental music plays.
Inside a living room, Monica and Chandler sit close together on a couch. Monica rests her head on Chandler's shoulder as the music fades.
Monica leans against Chandler's arm. "You know, when we move in together, can I get a gumball machine?" Chandler asks, looking toward her. Monica smiles and says, "Of course."
Monica looks up toward Chandler. "Joey wouldn't let you have one?" she asks.
Chandler looks forward, then back at Monica. "No, when it comes to sweets, he's surprisingly strict," he says. Audience laughter is audible.
Monica rests her head on Chandler's shoulder again as they remain sitting together.

Monica sits up slightly and turns her head toward Chandler. "Have you figured out a way to tell him you're moving?" she asks. Chandler gestures with both hands as he says, "No. No, I keep—know—and I can get out 'Joey, I have to—'"
Chandler continues, "But then I lose my nerve and I always finish with 'go to the bathroom.'" Audience laughter continues.
Chandler looks away from Monica briefly. "He may think I'm sick," he says, with his volume dropping on the last phrase.
Monica continues to watch Chandler as he turns his gaze back toward her.
Monica says, "You know, I really have to tell Rachel, but we just got to get it over with. You know what? The next time we see them, we're just going to tell them."
Monica looks at Chandler and asks, "Okay? That's it."
Chandler raises his eyebrows and says, "Oh, is that how this is going to work now? You're just going to order me around all the time?"
Monica smiles at him and says, "Pretty much."
Chandler nods his head and says, "All right."
The front door opens and Joey enters the room, wearing a grey long-sleeved shirt and black track pants with white stripes. The door slams shut behind him.
Joey walks toward the kitchen area. "Hey, Monica. Hey, man. You feeling better?" he asks. Monica and Chandler stand up from the couch.
Monica and Chandler walk toward the kitchen table where Joey stands.
Monica stands beside Chandler. "Joey, we have something to tell you," she says. Joey turns his body to face them.
Joey's eyes widen and he points both hands toward Monica. "Oh, my God, you're pregnant!" he shouts, his volume much higher than the previous lines.
Monica and Chandler both stare forward with eyes wide and mouths slightly open. Audience laughter is audible.
Chandler shakes his head and says, "No, no."
Monica lowers her gaze while Chandler keeps his eyes on Joey.
Chandler steps closer to Joey and says, "Look, Joe, here's the thing."
Joey stands still, looking at Chandler with his hands held at his sides.
Chandler gestures with his right hand toward Monica. "Monica and I have decided to live together here. So—" he says, pausing briefly.
Chandler looks directly at Joey. "I'm going to be moving out, man," he says, his volume softer than before.
Joey stands motionless with a flat facial expression, looking between Monica and Chandler. The sound of a car horn is audible in the distance.
Joey blinks his eyes and says, "Wow."
Joey's facial expression shifts into a smile and he steps forward. "Hey, really happy for you guys," he says, his volume rising. He briefly hugs Monica and Chandler simultaneously.
Joey releases the hug and turns toward the door. "Congratulations. See you later," he says, walking away.
Monica and Chandler watch Joey as he walks toward the door.
Monica follows Joey toward the doorway and asks, "Are you okay?"
Joey stops with his hand on the doorknob. "I've got to go. I've got an acting job," he says, speaking faster than his previous response.
Joey opens the door and steps into the hallway, then stops and turns back toward the room.
Joey looks at Monica and Chandler and says, "Like you'd believe that," with his volume lower.
Joey stands in the hallway with his shoulders slumped. Monica and Chandler stand in the doorway looking out at him.
Joey looks down at the floor. "This sucks," he says. Chandler steps toward him into the hallway and says, "Look, I'm just going to be right across the hall."
Chandler raises his right hand and says, "And I promise you the minute Monica and I break up, I'm moving right back in with you."
Monica turns her head to look at Chandler.
Monica looks at Chandler and says, "Maybe that's now." Chandler looks at her and she smiles. Joey steps back into the room and says, "Look, look. If you're going to be moving in with him—"
Joey points a finger at Chandler while looking at Monica. "I feel that it is my responsibility to tell you the truth about him. Okay? He's a terrible roommate. Terrible," he says.
Joey gestures emphatically with one hand. "He forgets to—" he starts, then pauses.
Joey's face remains tight as he looks away briefly.
Joey says, "Always—" then stops again.
Joey's eyes narrow and his mouth twitches slightly.
Joey's voice breaks as he says, "Oh, who am I kidding? He's the best roommate ever." He steps forward and hugs Chandler tightly.
Monica watches the hug, then turns and walks away. Audience laughter and clapping are audible.
The setting changes to an exterior view of a park with a large stone arch and city buildings in the background. Upbeat instrumental music plays.
Inside the Central Perk coffee shop, Ross enters the room wearing a green t-shirt. "Hey, Peeps," he says to Phoebe.

Phoebe sits on the orange couch reading a magazine. "Oh, look, here's an article about people who marry people and don't tell them," she says. Ross looks down as she adds, "Really? Yeah, I'm reading 'Ross Geller' magazine."
Monica enters Central Perk wearing a light purple shirt and walks toward the orange couch.
Monica leans on the back of the couch. "Hey, have you seen Rachel?" she asks Phoebe.
Phoebe looks up from her magazine toward Monica. "No. Why?" she asks. Monica smiles and says, "I have some pretty exciting news."
Phoebe's eyes widen as she looks at Monica.
Phoebe asks, "You're pregnant?" Monica's smile fades and her eyebrows draw together.
Monica shakes her head from side to side.
Monica says, "No. Chandler and I are moving in together." Phoebe smiles and says, "Oh, that's good. Wow. But now if you were pregnant, what would you name it? Hint: Phoebe."
Monica sits down on the couch next to Phoebe.
Ross approaches the couch holding a pink mug. "Hey," he says.
Monica and Phoebe both say, "Hi." Ross asks, "What's up?" as he starts to sit on a nearby armchair.
Ross takes a sip from his mug while looking at Monica.
Monica looks toward Ross and says, "Well, Chandler and I are moving in together." Ross lowers his mug and looks at her.
Ross blinks and says, "Oh, my God."
Ross gestures with his free hand. "My little sister and my best friend shackin' up," he says, his voice quality slightly strained.
Monica smiles at Ross while Phoebe watches the interaction.
Ross smiles and says, "That's great. That's great." He leans forward and kisses Monica on the side of her head.
Monica continues to smile as Ross pulls his head back.

Inside Central Perk, Monica (purple shirt over black top) and Joey (dark shirt) sit on an orange couch. Ross (green t-shirt) leans over them while Phoebe (pink tie-dye shirt) stands nearby. Background chatter and audience laughter are audible.
Ross leans in and hugs Monica. Smiling, Monica says, "I'm happy too. Okay, come here."
Joey joins the hug, pulling Ross and Monica toward him as Phoebe steps away.
Phoebe sits on the couch next to Monica and Joey. She says, "Wow. Big day, huh?"
Phoebe looks toward Ross and Monica and says, "People moving in. People getting annulled."
Ross holds a pink mug and drinks from it, his eyes widening. Audience laughter continues.
Monica stands up and begins walking toward the back of the room. "Anyway, I gotta go find Rachel," she says.
Monica continues walking away and adds, "But if you guys see her, would you please try to give her some really bad news so mine doesn't seem so..."
Phoebe watches Monica leave, then turns her gaze forward.
Phoebe looks at Ross and asks, "Hmm, something bad to tell Rachel?"
Ross continues to drink from the mug, his eyes remaining wide. Audience laughter is audible.
Phoebe says, "Bad news for Rachel. Look at that. Be." Her gaze remains on Ross.
Ross lowers the mug from his face and looks at Phoebe.
Ross gestures with his free hand and says, "Can you just lay off, please? All right? My life is an embarrassment. I should just go live under somebody's stairs."
Phoebe looks toward Ross, her mouth slightly open.
Phoebe looks back and forth between Ross and the rest of the room. "Ross, it's not that big a deal. So you'll have been divorced three times. You'll still have a life. You'll go on dates."
Ross says, "No, I won't. I'll be at the bottom of the dating barrel now. The only guys below me will be four divorce guy, murderer guy..."
Ross continues, "...and geologist." He raises both hands with his palms up. Audience laughter is heard.
Phoebe looks at Ross, her lips slightly parted.
Phoebe says, "Ross, you're being ridiculous, okay? You are cute and smart and sweet. And that is much more important than three stupid divorces."
Ross points a finger at Phoebe and says, "Oh, yeah? Have you ever dated anyone who's been divorced three times?"
Phoebe shakes her head slightly and says, "That's not really fair. You know, most guys have been divorced three times are like, 60."
Ross looks toward the coffee table in front of them.
Phoebe says, "You're young."
Ross remains still, looking ahead.
Ross nods slightly and says, "It's true. I am young."
Phoebe says, "Well," and begins to stand up from the couch.
Ross holds his mug as Phoebe stands up fully. Audience laughter is audible.
Phoebe says, "Ross, nobody cares about this except you. This embarrassment thing is all in your head."
Phoebe leans down and reaches for Ross's arm.
Phoebe says, "Here I'll show." She pulls Ross to his feet.
Phoebe leads Ross toward a small table where three women are sitting.
Phoebe says, "Hi," while Ross stands beside her.
One of the women at the table looks up at Phoebe and Ross.
Phoebe says, "Hi. Listen, my friend Ross is about to be divorced for the third time. But wouldn't you date him?" Ross stands with one hand on his chin.
Ross says, "And if you wait right here, I'll go get Ross," his volume slightly lower than Phoebe's preceding line. He points behind himself and begins to walk away.
Ross continues walking while Phoebe remains standing by the table. Audience laughter is heard.
In an apartment, Monica (purple shirt) stands in the living room. Chandler (navy suit, pink tie) enters through the front door. "Hey," he says.
Monica turns toward Chandler and says, "Ugh, I thought you were Rachel." Chandler asks, "What gave me away?"
Monica says, "I just got to tell her. I have to get it over with. I've told Ross and Phoebe, and she's the only one left." She gestures with both hands.
Chandler steps closer to Monica. "Okay, that's it. So everybody knows. It's official. We're moving in together. No turning back."
Chandler says, "Are you scared? Are you?" his volume rising and his pace becoming faster on the second "Are you?".
Monica crosses her arms over her chest and looks at Chandler.
Monica says, "No." Chandler smiles and says, "Wow. That was my scariest voice. You're very brave."
Monica smiles and leans in to hug Chandler. The front door opens and Rachel (white top, grey skirt) enters.
Rachel says, "Hey." Monica and Chandler pull apart.
Monica and Chandler say "Hey" together. Rachel sighs and says, "Ugh, the worst day, you know?"
Rachel continues, "You think you're finally making progress at work and then your boss calls you Raquel." She hangs her bag on a hook near the door.
Monica and Chandler stand near the doorway, watching Rachel.
Chandler says, "Hey, listen, for the first four years of my work, everybody called me Shah La..." He makes several repetitive vocal sounds at a rising pitch.
Rachel looks at Chandler while audience laughter is audible.
Rachel continues to look at Chandler while Monica stands beside him with her arms crossed.
Chandler asks, "Seriously?" and Rachel says, "I believe you."
Rachel turns her body toward Monica.
Rachel says, "So, it was right in the middle of a staff meeting, so of course, no one else wants to correct her, so everyone else is calling me Raquel." She gestures with her hands.
Rachel says, "By the end of the day, the mailroom guys were calling me Rocky." She places her hands on her hips.
Monica and Chandler look at each other.
Chandler looks at Rachel and says, "Well, I still think you're very, very nice and very pretty."
Rachel looks toward Chandler and asks, "What?"
Chandler says, "What?" his volume slightly lower than Rachel's.
Chandler says, "All yours, babe," and walks toward the back of the apartment.
Monica reaches out and takes Rachel's arm.
Monica leads Rachel toward the kitchen table and says, "Rachel, come here. Have a seat."
Rachel sits in a purple chair at the wooden table.
Monica stands by the table and says, "Okay."
Monica sits in a blue chair opposite Rachel and places her hands on the table. "Listen, Chandler and I are going to live together..."
Monica finishes her sentence with, "here," while maintaining eye contact with Rachel.
Monica keeps her gaze on Rachel, who remains seated in the chair. Audience laughter is audible.
Inside an apartment, Monica sits in a purple chair at a wooden table across from Rachel, who wears a white tank top and grey skirt.
Rachel brings her hands together and says, "Oh, my God," her volume soft and breathy.
Rachel begins clapping her hands together. Chandler, wearing a dark suit with a pink tie, leans against a doorway in the background.
Rachel continues clapping her hands and says, "that's so great."
Chandler stands up straight. Rachel pushes back her chair and stands up from the table.
Rachel steps toward Monica, who has also stood up, and wraps her arms around her. "I'm so happy for you guys," Rachel says.
Rachel and Monica continue their hug. Audience laughter is audible.
Rachel pulls back from the hug but keeps her hands on Monica's upper arms. Looking at Monica, she says, "Really. And that was so sweet of you to ask."
Rachel briefly touches her hair with both hands before lowering them.

Rachel raises her hands to her head and then extends them outward. "Oh, my God. The three of us are going to have such a good time moving together," she says, her volume rising. Audience laughter continues.
Monica's eyes widen as she looks toward Chandler. Chandler's smile remains but his gaze stays fixed on Rachel.
Chandler looks toward Rachel and says, "Yes, we are," his pitch flatter than Rachel's preceding line.
Rachel turns toward Chandler and points her right index finger at him.

Rachel speaks quickly, her right hand gesturing toward Chandler. "You're going to have to watch those long showers you take in the morning because, you know, Raquel can't be late." Audience laughter is audible.
Monica reaches out and touches Rachel’s arm. Chandler blinks and keeps his gaze on Rachel.
Monica looks at Rachel and says, "Yeah, he's going to work on that," her volume softer than Rachel's previous line.
Rachel raises both hands, palms facing outward at shoulder height.
Rachel says, "This is so exciting," her volume loud and pitch high.
Rachel turns and begins walking toward the hallway behind the kitchen.
Walking away from the table, Rachel says, "Oh, wow," her volume lower than her previous line.
Rachel stops near the doorway, turns back to face Monica and Chandler, and claps her hands once.
Rachel sings, "Come and knock on our door," while swaying her upper body slightly.
Monica points two fingers at her own eyes and then toward Chandler. Chandler remains leaning near the doorway.
Rachel continues singing, "we'll be waiting for you," then turns and exits through the doorway. Audience laughter is audible.
Monica turns to Chandler with her hands raised and mouth open. Chandler looks back at her and gestures with both hands.
The setting changes to a coffee shop. Instrumental guitar and drum music plays. Ross, wearing a green t-shirt, sits on a couch with four women, including Phoebe, who wears a pink patterned shirt.
Ross holds a white mug and says, "see, once you know the stories, it's not that bad."
Ross gestures with his palms up and says, "First marriage, wife's, hidden sexuality. Not my fault."
Three women on the couch opposite Ross—one in a brown floral shirt, one in white, and Meg in red—look at him. Audience laughter is audible.
Ross moves his hands side-to-side and says, "Second marriage, said the wrong name at the altar a little my fault."
Phoebe sits at a table nearby, resting her chin on her hand while watching Ross.

Ross gestures with his hands toward his face and then outward. "Third marriage, well, they really shouldn't allow you to get married when you're that drunk and have writing all over your face. Nevada's"
Ross finishes the sentence with, "fault."
Ross looks between the women on the couch. Meg, on the right, shifts her seating position.
Phoebe gestures toward Ross and says, "Okay, so what do you think, ladies? Who wouldn't be interested? Who wouldn't want to date him? Well, the divorces don't"
The woman in the brown floral shirt gestures with her hands as she speaks. "bother me. I'd date him, but not while he's still married. Okay, what about you? Wouldn't you want to date?"
The woman in the white shirt gestures toward herself and says, "Actually, I'm not dating at all anymore. See, I figured out that I was only dating guys that, like, bad for me."
The woman in the white shirt continues, "So I decided that until I could work that out and those demons and whatever."
Ross turns his head to look toward Meg.
Phoebe turns her head toward Meg and asks, "What about you, Meg?"
Meg looks at Ross and says, "Well, I don't care about the divorces either, but I wouldn't date him. Normally, he doesn't sweat this much." Audience laughter is audible.
Ross blinks and briefly looks down before looking back at Meg.
Meg maintains her gaze on Ross and says, "It's just that he's obviously still in love with this Rachel girl."
Ross says, "What?" his volume louder than the previous line. Phoebe leans closer to Ross and says, "She said he's obviously still in love with," her volume lower than Ross's.
Phoebe stands up from the table and walks away. Ross watches her move, his eyes wide and mouth slightly open.
Ross gestures emphatically with both hands in front of his chest. "this is crazy. I mean, yes, yes, Rachel is my good friend, and I have loved her in the past, but now she is"
Ross lowers his hands slightly and says, "just my wife." His volume is lower on this line.
Ross keeps his hands raised at chest level, looking toward the women on the couch. Audience laughter is audible.
The women continue to look at Ross as he maintains his posture. The scene fades to black.
